mnemonics.title: FILE OPEN MODES
mnemonics.content: READ: r/rb (must exist), WRITE: w/wb (create/truncate), APPEND: a/ab (create/add). PLUS: + adds both read/write. BINARY: b specifies binary mode.

mnemonics.title: FEOF CHECK PATTERN
mnemonics.content: TYPICAL WHILE LOOP: while ((ch = fgetc(file)) != EOF) { process ch; }. feof() should be checked AFTER a read operation returns EOF to distinguish EOF from error.

mnemonics.title: FILE FUNCTIONS BY TASK
mnemonics.content: OPEN: fopen(), CLOSE: fclose(), READ CHAR: fgetc/getc, WRITE CHAR: fputc/putc, READ STRING: fgets, WRITE STRING: fputs, FORMAT: fprintf/fscanf, POSITION: fseek/ftell/rewind, CHECK: feof/ferror

acronyms.usage: Returned by file reading functions to indicate no more data to read

acronyms.expansion: Structure containing information about a file stream
acronyms.usage: Used as FILE* pointer type for file operations
Default input stream, often used with file functions like getchar()
Default output stream, often used with printf()
Origin parameter in fseek() to position from start of file (value 0)
Origin parameter in fseek() to position from current location (value 1)
Origin parameter in fseek() to position from end of file (value 2)

visualTips.visualization: Think of a file as a long tape with a read/write head. The file position indicator is like the head position. fseek moves the head, ftell tells you where it is, rewind moves it to start.
A buffer is like a holding area between your program and disk. fflush empties the buffer to disk immediately. Without flushing, data might stay in buffer if program crashes.
Text mode: translator converts newline (\n) to carriage-return+line-feed (\r\n) on Windows. Binary mode: no conversion, raw bytes pass through. Picture a conversion filter in text mode.

analogies.analogy: Like reading a book from page 1 to end. You cannot jump to page 50 without physically moving through pages 1-49. To reread, you must close and reopen or use rewind().
r (read): You can only look at the book. w (write): You get a fresh notebook to write in (old one destroyed). a (append): You get a notebook where you can only add pages at the end.

analogies.analization: Think of FILE* as a remote control for the file. It tells functions which file to operate on, just like a TV remote tells which TV to control.
